ai_content_json.result: 在排除任务中AUC从0.914提升至0.942，在保留任务中从0.921提升至0.950，均达到新的最先进水平。
ai_content_json.conclusion: 所提出的深度学习框架结合新型标注方法显著提升了CAD-RADS自动评分性能，为临床诊断提供了高效可靠的工具。

tldr: 本文提出了一种基于深度学习和任务特定中心线标注的自动CAD-RADS评分方法，以提高冠状动脉疾病诊断的效率和准确性。
motivation: 冠状动脉疾病（CAD）是全球主要死亡原因之一，传统人工评估CAD-RADS评分耗时且主观性强，亟需自动化算法辅助诊断。
method: 采用基于严重程度的标签编码、测试时增强（TTA）和模型集成方法，并提出一种任务和模型特定的启发式冠状动脉分段标注技术，将冠状动脉树划分为患者间一致的区域。
result: 在排除任务中AUC从0.914提升至0.942，在保留任务中从0.921提升至0.950，均达到新的最先进水平。
conclusion: 所提出的深度学习框架结合新型标注方法显著提升了CAD-RADS自动评分性能，为临床诊断提供了高效可靠的工具。
search_text: CAD-RADS Scoring using Deep Learning and Task-Specific Centerline Labeling 使用深度学习和特定于任务的中心线标记进行 CAD-RADS 评分 With coronary artery disease (CAD) persisting to be one of the leading causes of death worldwide, interest in supporting physicians with algorithms to speed up and improve diagnosis is high. In clinical practice, the severeness of CAD is often assessed with a coronary CT angiography (CCTA) scan and manually graded with the CAD-Reporting and Data System (CAD-RADS) score. The clinical questions this score assesses are whether patients have CAD or not (rule-out) and whether they have severe CAD or not (hold-out). In this work, we reach new state-of-the-art performance for automatic CAD-RADS scoring. We propose using severity-based label encoding, test time augmentation (TTA) and model ensembling for a task-specific deep learning architecture. Furthermore, we introduce a novel task- and model-specific, heuristic coronary segment labeling, which subdivides coronary trees into consistent parts across patients. It is fast, robust, and easy to implement. We were able to raise the previously reported area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUC) from 0.914 to 0.942 in the rule-out and from 0.921 to 0.950 in the hold-out task respectively. 由于冠状动脉疾病 (CAD) 仍然是全球死亡的主要原因之一，因此人们对通过算法来支持医生加速和改进诊断的兴趣很高。在临床实践中，CAD 的严重程度通常通过冠状动脉 CT 血管造影 (CCTA) 扫描进行评估，并通过 CAD 报告和数据系统 (CAD-RADS) 评分进行手动分级。该评分评估的临床问题是患者是否患有 CAD（排除）以及患者是否患有严重 CAD（保留）。在这项工作中，我们实现了自动 CAD-RADS 评分的新的最先进性能。我们建议使用基于严重性的标签编码、测试时间增强（TTA）和模型集成来实现特定于任务的深度学习架构。此外，我们引入了一种新颖的特定于任务和模型的启发式冠状动脉节段标记，它将冠状动脉树细分为跨患者的一致部分。它快速、强大且易于实施。我们能够将先前报告的受试者工作特征曲线下面积 (AUC) 在排除任务中从 0.914 提高到 0.942，在保留任务中从 0.921 提高到 0.950。 本文提出了一种基于深度学习和任务特定中心线标注的自动CAD-RADS评分方法，以提高冠状动脉疾病诊断的效率和准确性。 Felix Denzinger,Michael Wels,Oliver Taubmann,Mehmet A. Gülsün,Max Schöbinger,Florian André,Sebastian J. Buss,Johannes Görich,Michael Sühling,Andreas Maier,Katharina Breininger eess.IV,cs.AI,cs.CV

authors_text: Shuhao Cao,Peng Xu,David A. Clifton
title: How to Understand Masked Autoencoders
title_zh: 如何理解屏蔽自动编码器
abstract: "Masked Autoencoders (MAE) Are Scalable Vision Learners" revolutionizes the self-supervised learning method in that it not only achieves the state-of-the-art for image pre-training, but is also a milestone that bridges the gap between visual and linguistic masked autoencoding (BERT-style) pre-trainings. However, to our knowledge, to date there are no theoretical perspectives to explain the powerful expressivity of MAE. In this paper, we, for the first time, propose a unified theoretical framework that provides a mathematical understanding for MAE. Specifically, we explain the patch-based attention approaches of MAE using an integral kernel under a non-overlapping domain decomposition setting. To help the research community to further comprehend the main reasons of the great success of MAE, based on our framework, we pose five questions and answer them with mathematical rigor using insights from operator theory.
summary: "Masked Autoencoders (MAE) Are Scalable Vision Learners" revolutionizes the self-supervised learning method in that it not only achieves the state-of-the-art for image pre-training, but is also a milestone that bridges the gap between visual and linguistic masked autoencoding (BERT-style) pre-trainings. However, to our knowledge, to date there are no theoretical perspectives to explain the powerful expressivity of MAE. In this paper, we, for the first time, propose a unified theoretical framework that provides a mathematical understanding for MAE. Specifically, we explain the patch-based attention approaches of MAE using an integral kernel under a non-overlapping domain decomposition setting. To help the research community to further comprehend the main reasons of the great success of MAE, based on our framework, we pose five questions and answer them with mathematical rigor using insights from operator theory.
abstract_zh: “掩模自动编码器（MAE）是可扩展的视觉学习器”彻底改变了自监督学习方法，它不仅实现了图像预训练的最先进水平，而且也是弥合视觉和语言掩模自动编码（BERT 式）预训练之间差距的里程碑。然而，据我们所知，迄今为止还没有理论视角来解释 MAE 强大的表达能力。在本文中，我们首次提出了一个统一的理论框架，为 MAE 提供了数学理解。具体来说，我们在非重叠域分解设置下使用积分内核解释了基于补丁的 MAE 注意力方法。为了帮助研究界进一步理解 MAE 取得巨大成功的主要原因，基于我们的框架，我们提出了五个问题，并利用算子理论的见解以数学严谨的方式回答了这些问题。

AI.tldr: 提出统一的数学理论框架解释MAE的表达能力
AI.motivation: 现有研究缺乏对MAE强大表达能力的理论解释，需建立数学框架以深化理解
AI.method: 基于非重叠域分解的积分核模型，结合算子理论进行数学分析
AI.result: 构建理论框架并严格解答五个关键问题，揭示MAE成功的核心因素
AI.conclusion: 建立MAE的数学理论基础，为后续研究提供理论支持

authors_text: Bryon Tjanaka,Matthew C. Fontaine,Julian Togelius,Stefanos Nikolaidis
title: Approximating Gradients for Differentiable Quality Diversity in Reinforcement Learning
title_zh: 强化学习中可微质量多样性的近似梯度
comment: Published as a conference paper at the 2022 Genetic and Evolutionary Computation Conference (GECCO '22); Online article available at http://dqd-rl.github.io
comments: Published as a conference paper at the 2022 Genetic and Evolutionary Computation Conference (GECCO '22); Online article available at http://dqd-rl.github.io
abstract: Consider the problem of training robustly capable agents. One approach is to generate a diverse collection of agent polices. Training can then be viewed as a quality diversity (QD) optimization problem, where we search for a collection of performant policies that are diverse with respect to quantified behavior. Recent work shows that differentiable quality diversity (DQD) algorithms greatly accelerate QD optimization when exact gradients are available. However, agent policies typically assume that the environment is not differentiable. To apply DQD algorithms to training agent policies, we must approximate gradients for performance and behavior. We propose two variants of the current state-of-the-art DQD algorithm that compute gradients via approximation methods common in reinforcement learning (RL). We evaluate our approach on four simulated locomotion tasks. One variant achieves results comparable to the current state-of-the-art in combining QD and RL, while the other performs comparably in two locomotion tasks. These results provide insight into the limitations of current DQD algorithms in domains where gradients must be approximated. Source code is available at https://github.com/icaros-usc/dqd-rl
summary: Consider the problem of training robustly capable agents. One approach is to generate a diverse collection of agent polices. Training can then be viewed as a quality diversity (QD) optimization problem, where we search for a collection of performant policies that are diverse with respect to quantified behavior. Recent work shows that differentiable quality diversity (DQD) algorithms greatly accelerate QD optimization when exact gradients are available. However, agent policies typically assume that the environment is not differentiable. To apply DQD algorithms to training agent policies, we must approximate gradients for performance and behavior. We propose two variants of the current state-of-the-art DQD algorithm that compute gradients via approximation methods common in reinforcement learning (RL). We evaluate our approach on four simulated locomotion tasks. One variant achieves results comparable to the current state-of-the-art in combining QD and RL, while the other performs comparably in two locomotion tasks. These results provide insight into the limitations of current DQD algorithms in domains where gradients must be approximated. Source code is available at https://github.com/icaros-usc/dqd-rl
abstract_zh: 考虑训练能力强的代理的问题。一种方法是生成多样化的代理策略集合。然后，训练可以被视为质量多样性（QD）优化问题，我们在其中搜索在量化行为方面多样化的绩效策略的集合。最近的研究表明，当精确梯度可用时，可微分质量多样性 (DQD) 算法可以极大地加速 QD 优化。然而，代理策略通常假设环境是不可微的。要将 DQD 算法应用于训练代理策略，我们必须近似性能和行为的梯度。我们提出了当前最先进的 DQD 算法的两种变体，它们通过强化学习 (RL) 中常见的近似方法来计算梯度。我们在四个模拟运动任务上评估我们的方法。一种变体在结合 QD 和 RL 方面取得了与当前最先进的结果相当的结果，而另一种变体在两项运动任务中的表现相当。这些结果让我们深入了解当前 DQD 算法在梯度必须近似的领域中的局限性。源代码位于 https://github.com/icaros-usc/dqd-rl

AI.tldr: 本文提出两种改进的微分质量多样性（DQD）算法，通过近似方法解决强化学习中环境不可微分时的梯度计算问题，以生成多样化的高性能智能体策略。
AI.motivation: 传统DQD算法依赖精确梯度，但智能体策略常面临环境不可微分的挑战，需通过近似方法实现梯度计算以提升QD优化效率。
AI.method: 设计两种基于强化学习常见近似梯度方法的DQD算法变体，在四个模拟运动任务中验证其有效性。
AI.result: 一种变体达到当前QD与RL结合的最先进水平，另一种在两个任务中表现相当，揭示了近似梯度下DQD算法的局限性。
AI.conclusion: 近似梯度方法可应用于DQD框架，但现有算法在需梯度近似的领域仍存在局限，为未来研究提供方向。

authors_text: Søren Taverniers,Svyatoslav Korneev,Kyle M. Pietrzyk,Morad Behandish
title: Accelerating Part-Scale Simulation in Liquid Metal Jet Additive Manufacturing via Operator Learning
title_zh: 通过操作员学习加速液态金属喷射增材制造的部分规模仿真
abstract: Predicting part quality for additive manufacturing (AM) processes requires high-fidelity numerical simulation of partial differential equations (PDEs) governing process multiphysics on a scale of minimum manufacturable features. This makes part-scale predictions computationally demanding, especially when they require many small-scale simulations. We consider drop-on-demand liquid metal jetting (LMJ) as an illustrative example of such computational complexity. A model describing droplet coalescence for LMJ may include coupled incompressible fluid flow, heat transfer, and phase change equations. Numerically solving these equations becomes prohibitively expensive when simulating the build process for a full part consisting of thousands to millions of droplets. Reduced-order models (ROMs) based on neural networks (NN) or k-nearest neighbor (kNN) algorithms have been built to replace the original physics-based solver and are computationally tractable for part-level simulations. However, their quick inference capabilities often come at the expense of accuracy, robustness, and generalizability. We apply an operator learning (OL) approach to learn a mapping between initial and final states of the droplet coalescence process for enabling rapid and accurate part-scale build simulation. Preliminary results suggest that OL requires order-of-magnitude fewer data points than a kNN approach and is generalizable beyond the training set while achieving similar prediction error.

We apply an operator learning (OL) approach to learn a mapping between initial and final states of the droplet coalescence process for enabling rapid and accurate part-scale build simulation. Preliminary results suggest that OL requires order-of-magnitude fewer data points than a kNN approach and is generalizable beyond the training set while achieving similar prediction error.
abstract_zh: 预测增材制造 (AM) 工艺的零件质量需要对偏微分方程 (PDE) 进行高保真数值模拟，在最小可制造特征的范围内控制工艺多物理场。这使得部分规模的预测在计算上要求很高，特别是当它们需要许多小规模的模拟时。我们将按需滴液液态金属喷射（LMJ）视为这种计算复杂性的一个说明性示例。描述 LMJ 液滴聚结的模型可能包括耦合的不可压缩流体流动、传热和相变方程。当模拟由数千到数百万个液滴组成的完整零件的构建过程时，以数值方式求解这些方程变得非常昂贵。基于神经网络 (NN) 或 k 最近邻 (kNN) 算法的降阶模型 (ROM) 已被构建来取代原始的基于物理的求解器，并且在计算上易于处理部分级仿真。然而，它们的快速推理能力通常是以牺牲准确性、鲁棒性和普遍性为代价的。我们应用算子学习（OL）方法来学习液滴聚结过程的初始状态和最终状态之间的映射，以实现快速、准确的部分规模构建模拟。初步结果表明，OL 需要比 kNN 方法少几个数量级的数据点，并且可以推广到训练集之外，同时实现类似的预测误差。

AI.tldr: 本文通过算子学习加速液态金属喷射增材制造中的零件尺度仿真。
AI.motivation: 传统物理仿真计算量大且难以满足零件级大规模仿真需求，现有降阶模型在速度与精度间存在权衡问题。
AI.method: 采用算子学习方法建立液滴融合过程的初始状态到最终状态的映射关系，替代传统物理求解器。
AI.result: 算子学习方法相比kNN算法减少数量级数据需求，保持相似预测误差且具有更好的泛化能力。
AI.conclusion: 算子学习为增材制造零件级仿真提供了兼顾速度与精度的新范式。

search_text: Accelerating Part-Scale Simulation in Liquid Metal Jet Additive Manufacturing via Operator Learning 通过操作员学习加速液态金属喷射增材制造的部分规模仿真 Predicting part quality for additive manufacturing (AM) processes requires high-fidelity numerical simulation of partial differential equations (PDEs) governing process multiphysics on a scale of minimum manufacturable features. This makes part-scale predictions computationally demanding, especially when they require many small-scale simulations. We consider drop-on-demand liquid metal jetting (LMJ) as an illustrative example of such computational complexity. A model describing droplet coalescence for LMJ may include coupled incompressible fluid flow, heat transfer, and phase change equations. Numerically solving these equations becomes prohibitively expensive when simulating the build process for a full part consisting of thousands to millions of droplets. Reduced-order models (ROMs) based on neural networks (NN) or k-nearest neighbor (kNN) algorithms have been built to replace the original physics-based solver and are computationally tractable for part-level simulations. However, their quick inference capabilities often come at the expense of accuracy, robustness, and generalizability. We apply an operator learning (OL) approach to learn a mapping between initial and final states of the droplet coalescence process for enabling rapid and accurate part-scale build simulation. Preliminary results suggest that OL requires order-of-magnitude fewer data points than a kNN approach and is generalizable beyond the training set while achieving similar prediction error. 预测增材制造 (AM) 工艺的零件质量需要对偏微分方程 (PDE) 进行高保真数值模拟，在最小可制造特征的范围内控制工艺多物理场。这使得部分规模的预测在计算上要求很高，特别是当它们需要许多小规模的模拟时。我们将按需滴液液态金属喷射（LMJ）视为这种计算复杂性的一个说明性示例。描述 LMJ 液滴聚结的模型可能包括耦合的不可压缩流体流动、传热和相变方程。当模拟由数千到数百万个液滴组成的完整零件的构建过程时，以数值方式求解这些方程变得非常昂贵。基于神经网络 (NN) 或 k 最近邻 (kNN) 算法的降阶模型 (ROM) 已被构建来取代原始的基于物理的求解器，并且在计算上易于处理部分级仿真。然而，它们的快速推理能力通常是以牺牲准确性、鲁棒性和普遍性为代价的。我们应用算子学习（OL）方法来学习液滴聚结过程的初始状态和最终状态之间的映射，以实现快速、准确的部分规模构建模拟。初步结果表明，OL 需要比 kNN 方法少几个数量级的数据点，并且可以推广到训练集之外，同时实现类似的预测误差。 本文通过算子学习加速液态金属喷射增材制造中的零件尺度仿真。 Søren Taverniers,Svyatoslav Korneev,Kyle M. Pietrzyk,Morad Behandish physics.flu-dyn,cs.AI,cs.LG,physics.comp-ph,physics.data-an

authors_text: Cinjon Resnick,Or Litany,Amlan Kar,Karsten Kreis,James Lucas,Kyunghyun Cho,Sanja Fidler
title: Causal Scene BERT: Improving object detection by searching for challenging groups of data
title_zh: 因果场景 BERT：通过搜索具有挑战性的数据组来改进目标检测
abstract: Modern computer vision applications rely on learning-based perception modules parameterized with neural networks for tasks like object detection. These modules frequently have low expected error overall but high error on atypical groups of data due to biases inherent in the training process. In building autonomous vehicles (AV), this problem is an especially important challenge because their perception modules are crucial to the overall system performance. After identifying failures in AV, a human team will comb through the associated data to group perception failures that share common causes. More data from these groups is then collected and annotated before retraining the model to fix the issue. In other words, error groups are found and addressed in hindsight. Our main contribution is a pseudo-automatic method to discover such groups in foresight by performing causal interventions on simulated scenes. To keep our interventions on the data manifold, we utilize masked language models. We verify that the prioritized groups found via intervention are challenging for the object detector and show that retraining with data collected from these groups helps inordinately compared to adding more IID data. We also plan to release software to run interventions in simulated scenes, which we hope will benefit the causality community.
summary: Modern computer vision applications rely on learning-based perception modules parameterized with neural networks for tasks like object detection. These modules frequently have low expected error overall but high error on atypical groups of data due to biases inherent in the training process. In building autonomous vehicles (AV), this problem is an especially important challenge because their perception modules are crucial to the overall system performance. After identifying failures in AV, a human team will comb through the associated data to group perception failures that share common causes. More data from these groups is then collected and annotated before retraining the model to fix the issue. In other words, error groups are found and addressed in hindsight. Our main contribution is a pseudo-automatic method to discover such groups in foresight by performing causal interventions on simulated scenes. To keep our interventions on the data manifold, we utilize masked language models. We verify that the prioritized groups found via intervention are challenging for the object detector and show that retraining with data collected from these groups helps inordinately compared to adding more IID data. We also plan to release software to run interventions in simulated scenes, which we hope will benefit the causality community.
abstract_zh: 现代计算机视觉应用依赖于基于学习的感知模块，该模块通过神经网络参数化来完成物体检测等任务。这些模块通常总体上具有较低的预期误差，但由于训练过程中固有的偏差，非典型数据组的误差较高。在构建自动驾驶汽车（AV）时，这个问题是一个特别重要的挑战，因为它们的感知模块对于整体系统性能至关重要。在识别 AV 中的故障后，人类团队将梳理相关数据，对具有共同原因的感知故障进行分组。然后，在重新训练模型以解决问题之前，收集并注释来自这些组的更多数据。换句话说，错误组是事后发现并解决的。我们的主要贡献是一种伪自动方法，通过对模拟场景进行因果干预来预见性地发现此类群体。为了保持对数据流形的干预，我们使用屏蔽语言模型。我们验证了通过干预找到的优先组对于对象检测器来说是具有挑战性的，并且表明与添加更多 IID 数据相比，使用从这些组收集的数据进行重新训练有极大的帮助。我们还计划发布软件来在模拟场景中进行干预，我们希望这将使因果关系社区受益。

AI.tldr: 提出一种基于因果干预的伪自动方法，通过识别具有挑战性的数据组来提升目标检测性能，特别是在自动驾驶场景中。
AI.motivation: 现有目标检测模型在整体误差较低时，仍会在特殊数据组上出现高误差，而人工分析错误数据组效率低且滞后，亟需自动化方法提前发现并解决该问题。
AI.method: 利用因果干预在模拟场景中生成数据，并结合掩码语言模型保持数据流形，从而伪自动识别出对目标检测具有挑战性的数据组。
AI.result: 验证了通过干预发现的优先数据组显著提升检测性能，且再训练效果优于增加独立同分布数据；计划开源相关软件以促进因果推理研究。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效提升了模型对偏差数据的鲁棒性，为自动驾驶等安全关键领域提供新思路，并可能推动因果推理工具的普及。

authors_text: Fan Yu,Shiliang Zhang,Pengcheng Guo,Yihui Fu,Zhihao Du,Siqi Zheng,Weilong Huang,Lei Xie,Zheng-Hua Tan,DeLiang Wang,Yanmin Qian,Kong Aik Lee,Zhijie Yan,Bin Ma,Xin Xu,Hui Bu
title: Summary On The ICASSP 2022 Multi-Channel Multi-Party Meeting Transcription Grand Challenge
title_zh: ICASSP 2022多渠道多方会议转录大挑战赛总结
abstract: The ICASSP 2022 Multi-channel Multi-party Meeting Transcription Grand Challenge (M2MeT) focuses on one of the most valuable and the most challenging scenarios of speech technologies. The M2MeT challenge has particularly set up two tracks, speaker diarization (track 1) and multi-speaker automatic speech recognition (ASR) (track 2). Along with the challenge, we released 120 hours of real-recorded Mandarin meeting speech data with manual annotation, including far-field data collected by 8-channel microphone array as well as near-field data collected by each participants' headset microphone. We briefly describe the released dataset, track setups, baselines and summarize the challenge results and major techniques used in the submissions.
summary: The ICASSP 2022 Multi-channel Multi-party Meeting Transcription Grand Challenge (M2MeT) focuses on one of the most valuable and the most challenging scenarios of speech technologies. The M2MeT challenge has particularly set up two tracks, speaker diarization (track 1) and multi-speaker automatic speech recognition (ASR) (track 2). Along with the challenge, we released 120 hours of real-recorded Mandarin meeting speech data with manual annotation, including far-field data collected by 8-channel microphone array as well as near-field data collected by each participants' headset microphone. We briefly describe the released dataset, track setups, baselines and summarize the challenge results and major techniques used in the submissions.
abstract_zh: ICASSP 2022 多通道多方会议转录大挑战赛（M2MeT）专注于语音技术最有价值和最具挑战性的场景之一。 M2MeT挑战赛特别设置了两个赛道，说话人分类（赛道1）和多说话人自动语音识别（ASR）（赛道2）。伴随挑战赛，我们还发布了120小时真实录制的普通话会议语音数据并进行了手动标注，包括8通道麦克风阵列收集的远场数据以及每个参与者的耳机麦克风收集的近场数据。我们简要描述了已发布的数据集、跟踪设置、基线，并总结了挑战结果和提交中使用的主要技术。

AI.tldr: 本文介绍了ICASSP 2022多通道多方会议转录挑战赛（M2MeT），包含说话人验证和多说话人语音识别两个赛道，并发布了120小时中文会议语音数据集。
AI.motivation: 多通道多方会议场景下的语音转录是语音技术中的核心难题，具有重要研究价值，但缺乏高质量数据集和基准测试。
AI.method: 发布包含远场麦克风阵列和近场耳机麦克风录制的120小时真实中文会议数据，设置说话人验证与多说话人ASR两个赛道，提供基线模型并评估参赛方案。
AI.result: 总结了参赛团队在两个赛道上的性能表现，分析了主流技术方法如多说话人分离、声学模型优化等的应用效果。
AI.conclusion: 该挑战赛推动了多通道多方会议转录技术的发展，发布的数据集和评估框架为后续研究提供了重要基准。

search_text: Summary On The ICASSP 2022 Multi-Channel Multi-Party Meeting Transcription Grand Challenge ICASSP 2022多渠道多方会议转录大挑战赛总结 The ICASSP 2022 Multi-channel Multi-party Meeting Transcription Grand Challenge (M2MeT) focuses on one of the most valuable and the most challenging scenarios of speech technologies. The M2MeT challenge has particularly set up two tracks, speaker diarization (track 1) and multi-speaker automatic speech recognition (ASR) (track 2). Along with the challenge, we released 120 hours of real-recorded Mandarin meeting speech data with manual annotation, including far-field data collected by 8-channel microphone array as well as near-field data collected by each participants' headset microphone. We briefly describe the released dataset, track setups, baselines and summarize the challenge results and major techniques used in the submissions. ICASSP 2022 多通道多方会议转录大挑战赛（M2MeT）专注于语音技术最有价值和最具挑战性的场景之一。 M2MeT挑战赛特别设置了两个赛道，说话人分类（赛道1）和多说话人自动语音识别（ASR）（赛道2）。伴随挑战赛，我们还发布了120小时真实录制的普通话会议语音数据并进行了手动标注，包括8通道麦克风阵列收集的远场数据以及每个参与者的耳机麦克风收集的近场数据。我们简要描述了已发布的数据集、跟踪设置、基线，并总结了挑战结果和提交中使用的主要技术。 本文介绍了ICASSP 2022多通道多方会议转录挑战赛（M2MeT），包含说话人验证和多说话人语音识别两个赛道，并发布了120小时中文会议语音数据集。 Fan Yu,Shiliang Zhang,Pengcheng Guo,Yihui Fu,Zhihao Du,Siqi Zheng,Weilong Huang,Lei Xie,Zheng-Hua Tan,DeLiang Wang,Yanmin Qian,Kong Aik Lee,Zhijie Yan,Bin Ma,Xin Xu,Hui Bu cs.SD,eess.AS
